methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

transformations: ETH -> WETH wrapping if msg.value > 0
amount overridden by address(this).balance for ETH deposits
USDC deposited to Aave variable pool, scaled aToken minted
WETH transferred, szETH collateralToken minted 1:1
manipulation_risks: In multicall, address(this).balance is used instead of msg.value; first deposit in multicall consumes entire contract ETH balance including donations
Second ETH deposit in multicall would have amount=0 after first consumed all balance, but validation passed with params.amount>0

transformations: APR from yield curve -> ratePerTenor via aprToRatePerTenor
cashAmountIn -> creditAmountOut or creditAmountOut -> cashAmountIn via fee/rate calculations
Debt token minted to borrower, credit position created for lender
data_flows.trust_changes: Lender's borrowAToken transferred; debt position creates obligation for borrower
manipulation_risks: Borrower's borrow offer rate is used - rate could change between tx submission and execution

transformations: creditAmountIn -> cashAmountOut via rate and fee calculations
Self-debt created when RESERVED_ID, then credit transferred to lender
data_flows.trust_changes: Lender's funds transferred to borrower; lender gets credit claim
manipulation_risks: Fee inconsistency between getCashAmountOut and getCreditAmountIn: swap fee base differs (maxCashAmountOut vs cashAmountOut)

transformations: debtPosition.futureValue -> debtInCollateralToken via price oracle
Liquidation reward = min(surplus collateral, futureValue * rewardPercent / PERCENT) -- UNIT MISMATCH
Protocol profit from collateral remainder
data_flows.trust_changes: Borrower loses collateral; debt is cleared
exposure_risks: Oracle price manipulation could trigger premature liquidation
manipulation_risks: CRITICAL: Liquidation reward uses futureValue (borrow token units, e.g. 6 decimals) compared with collateral token amounts (18 decimals) - reward is negligible, disincentivizing liquidations

path: Fragmentation fee in collateral if credit split occurs
transformations: Credit from one position used to reduce debt on another
amountToCompensate = min(amount, credit1, credit2)
fragmentationFee converted from borrow token to collateral token units
data_flows.trust_changes: Borrower's debt reduced; lender's credit reassigned to new debt
manipulation_risks: Fragmentation fee in collateral capped by user balance - user can minimize collateral before compensating to reduce fee (constrained by underwater check)
With RESERVED_ID, creates self-debt without swap fee, potentially gaming around normal lending fees

transformations: String key -> state variable update
Address values via uint160 cast
data_flows.trust_changes: Protocol parameters changed - affects all future operations
exposure_risks: Admin can change priceFeed, feeRecipient to arbitrary addresses
manipulation_risks: crLiquidation can ONLY decrease (never increase) due to check: params.value >= state.riskConfig.crLiquidation
No validation on liquidationRewardPercent upper bound
swapFeeAPR, fragmentationFee, liquidationRewardPercent not bounded during initialization

transformations: claimAmount = credit * currentLiquidityIndex / liquidityIndexAtRepayment (captures Aave yield between repay and claim)
manipulation_risks: No msg.sender check - anyone can trigger claim, but funds go to creditPosition.lender

path: Reactivate debt position with new borrower
borrowAToken.transferFrom to new borrower and feeRecipient
transformations: Original debt fully repaid then reissued to new borrower
issuanceValue = futureValue * PERCENT / (PERCENT + ratePerTenor)
liquidatorProfitBorrowToken = futureValue - issuanceValue (no swap fee charged)
data_flows.trust_changes: Debt position transferred to new borrower; credit positions unchanged
manipulation_risks: KEEPER-only but the entire interest spread goes to feeRecipient with no swap fee - potential value extraction
Inherits the liquidation reward unit mismatch bug

core_rules: SUM(credit positions per debt) should equal debt position futureValue (for ACTIVE loans)
Borrowers must maintain CR above crOpening when creating new debt
Credit positions are only transferrable when loan is ACTIVE and borrower is not underwater
Minimum credit amount enforced on creation and after reduction (0 is allowed for claims)
Non-transferrable tokens: only Size contract can mint/burn/transfer
economic_incentives: Swap fee: APR-based fee proportional to tenor, charged on cash amount
Fragmentation fee: flat fee in borrow token terms, charged when credit is split
Liquidation reward: percentage of futureValue given to liquidator (BUGGED - wrong units)
Protocol liquidation fee: percentage of collateral remainder on profitable liquidations
Aave yield: borrowAToken accrues yield between deposit and withdrawal/claim
governance_mechanisms: BORROW_RATE_UPDATER_ROLE: update variable pool borrow rate

edge_cases.scenario: Liquidation reward calculation uses futureValue in borrow token units (e.g., 6 decimals for USDC) but compares with collateral token surplus (18 decimals for WETH)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Liquidator receives meaningful reward (e.g., 5% of debt value in collateral terms)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Reward is ~50e6 in collateral units when it should be ~0.025e18, making it 500 billion times too small. Liquidator effectively gets zero profit beyond debt repayment value
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Liquidations become unprofitable, leading to no incentive to liquidate. Bad debt accumulates. Borrowers can be undercollateralized without consequences since no one will liquidate them.

edge_cases.scenario: UpdateConfig crLiquidation can only decrease, never increase
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Admin should be able to increase crLiquidation up to (but not including) crOpening
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Check `params.value >= state.riskConfig.crLiquidation` prevents any increase. Once lowered, cannot be raised.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If admin accidentally sets crLiquidation too low, protocol becomes less safe. Only recourse is full contract upgrade.

edge_cases.scenario: Fee calculation inconsistency between getCashAmountOut and getCreditAmountIn
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Swap fee should be based on the same base amount (maxCashAmountOut / present value) in both directions
edge_cases.actual_behavior: getCashAmountOut bases swap fee on maxCashAmountOut; getCreditAmountIn bases swap fee on cashAmountOut (which is lower). Protocol collects less fee when getCreditAmountIn is used.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Users can use SellCreditMarket with exactAmountIn=false to consistently pay lower fees than the equivalent getCashAmountOut path, causing protocol fee leakage.

edge_cases.scenario: Deposit via ETH in multicall uses address(this).balance instead of msg.value
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only user-sent ETH should be deposited
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Any ETH sitting in contract (from selfdestruct, coinbase tx) is included in deposit. Second ETH deposit in multicall deposits 0 after first consumed all balance.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Could capture donated ETH. Second deposit in multicall silently deposits 0 despite passing validation.

edge_cases.scenario: SellCreditMarket event emits params.tenor as dueDate parameter
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Event should emit actual dueDate (block.timestamp + tenor for new loans)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Emits params.tenor twice: once for tenor field, once for dueDate field
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Off-chain monitoring and indexing systems get incorrect dueDate data.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Variable rate features should either work or be clearly disabled
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Any yield curve with non-zero marketRateMultiplier will always revert with STALE_RATE when interval is 0
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Users who set marketRateMultipliers in their offers find them non-executable. Not an exploit but confusing behavior.

edge_cases.scenario: Compensate with RESERVED_ID creates self-debt at zero cost
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Creating new debt should involve some fee or constraint
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Borrower creates self-debt (lender=borrower) with no swap fee, only constrained by CR and minimum credit checks. This effectively moves debt from one position to a new one.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Borrowers can restructure their debt without paying swap fees by compensating with RESERVED_ID. The lender's credit gets moved to a new debt position.

edge_cases.scenario: No upper bound validation on liquidationRewardPercent during initialization or update
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Can be set to arbitrarily high values. Combined with the unit mismatch bug, even extreme values produce negligible rewards.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Admin misconfiguration could cause unexpected behavior if unit mismatch bug is fixed without also adding bounds.

edge_cases.scenario: Self-liquidation requires CR < PERCENT (100%) which is stricter than crLiquidation check
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Self-liquidation should be available when borrower is underwater (CR < crLiquidation)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Additional check `collateralRatio(debtPosition.borrower) >= PERCENT` prevents self-liquidation when CR is between PERCENT and crLiquidation. Self-liquidation only available when actually at loss.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Lenders cannot self-liquidate when borrower is underwater but CR > 100%. They must wait for regular liquidation which has broken incentives due to reward bug.

edge_cases.scenario: Claim can be called by anyone, not just the lender
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Possibly restrict to lender for access control
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Anyone can call claim for any repaid credit position. Funds always go to the lender, so no fund loss, but could be used for griefing (forced claiming).
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Third party can force claim on behalf of a lender, potentially impacting their Aave yield accumulation timing.

assumption_violations: The liquidation reward formula assumes futureValue and collateral amounts are in the same unit denomination - they are not when borrow and collateral tokens have different decimals
The crLiquidation update check assumes monotonic decrease is desirable - admin may need to increase it
The protocol assumes liquidators will be economically incentivized - the reward bug breaks this assumption
variablePoolBorrowRateStaleRateInterval=0 implicitly assumes no variable rate usage, but the code structure suggests it should be configurable
game_theory_risks: With negligible liquidation rewards, rational liquidators will not liquidate, allowing bad debt to accumulate
Borrowers can approach crLiquidation with low risk since liquidation is disincentivized
LiquidateWithReplacement sends all interest spread to feeRecipient - keeper has incentive only from collateral profit (which is also broken by reward bug)
Compensate fragmentation fee capped by collateral balance means borrowers near underwater threshold pay minimal fees
Fee asymmetry between buy and sell credit market paths creates arbitrage opportunity between paths
integration_risks: Aave variable pool could pause withdrawals, leaving borrowAToken holders unable to withdraw
Price oracle (Chainlink) could return stale or manipulated prices
Sequencer uptime feed only checked for L2s - L1 deployments skip this check
WETH wrapper is trusted - non-standard WETH could cause issues
Aave liquidity index changes between operations could cause slight accounting discrepancies

calculation_errors.error: Math.mulDivUp(debtPosition.futureValue, state.feeConfig.liquidationRewardPercent, PERCENT) produces a value in borrow token units (6 decimals for USDC) that is compared via Math.min with collateral token surplus (18 decimals for WETH). The reward is effectively 10^-12 of what it should be.

fees = Math.mulDivUp(cashAmountOut, swapFeePercent, PERCENT) uses cashAmountOut as base, but getCashAmountOut uses maxCashAmountOut as base for the same fee calculation. Inconsistent fee computation across inverse functions.
calculation_errors.correct_formula: Fee should be based on the gross cash amount (present value of credit), not the net cash amount after fees

token_semantics.notes: Underlying borrow token. Standard USDC with Circle blacklist/pause capabilities.
Underlying collateral token. Standard WETH wrapper.
Non-transferrable scaled token wrapping Aave aUSDC. balanceOf returns unscaled amount. Only Size contract can transfer. transferFrom takes unscaled value, internally operates on scaled amounts.
Non-transferrable 1:1 wrapper for WETH collateral. Only Size contract can transfer.
Non-transferrable debt tracking token. Minted on debt creation, burned on repayment. Only Size contract can mint/burn.

invariants.name: credit_debt_conservation
state_vars: SUM(creditPositions[i].credit for all i pointing to debtPositionId)
invariants.notes: For ACTIVE loans, sum of credit should equal debt futureValue. For REPAID loans, credit can be less as lenders claim.

invariants.name: collateral_solvency
invariants.notes: Protocol should hold at least as much underlying collateral as szETH total supply.

invariants.name: borrow_atoken_backing
invariants.notes: Protocol's Aave aToken balance should back the total supply of borrowAToken (in scaled terms).

invariants.name: cr_opening_gt_cr_liquidation
invariants.notes: crOpening must always be strictly greater than crLiquidation. Enforced by Initialize validation.

invariants.name: swap_fee_bounded
invariants.notes: swapFeeAPR * maxTenor / YEAR < PERCENT must hold to prevent fee exceeding 100%
